title: Interior Mutability Pattern
description: Mutate data even when you only have a shared (&T) reference. Normally, shared references in Rust are immutable. Interior mutability types like Cell, RefCell, Mutex, and RwLock move borrow checking from compile time to runtime, enabling mutation through shared references. This is essential for shared state, caches, and reference-counted data.
whenToUse: Shared mutable state behind Rc or Arc
When the borrow checker is too conservative for your access pattern
Counters, loggers, or observers that need to mutate through shared refs
realWorldUse: Rc<RefCell<T>> for shared mutable data in single-threaded code. Arc<Mutex<T>> for shared mutable data across threads. OnceLock/LazyLock for lazy initialization. std::cell::OnceCell for single-assignment values.
